name: Leinart Bankruptcy Law Firm - Fort Worth
description_short: Fort Worth-based bankruptcy law firm offering Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filing services, plus personal injury representation. 24/7 consultation availability.
description_long:
Leinart Law Firm is a Fort Worth, Texas-based legal practice specializing in bankruptcy and personal injury cases. The firm positions itself as a full-service solution for individuals facing financial distress or accident-related claims, operating with 24/7 live call answering to accommodate clients in urgent situations.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy services including Chapter 7 liquidation bankruptcy, Chapter 13 repayment plan bankruptcy, and business bankruptcy filing. Beyond filing, they assist with related financial crises: foreclosure defense, repossession prevention, wage garnishment stops, and debt discharge across credit cards, medical bills, and tax debt. The firm also handles personal injury claims from car accidents, slip-and-fall incidents, semi-truck accidents, wrongful death, and medical malpractice cases.

Leinart distinguishes itself through its dual-practice model combining bankruptcy and personal injury law under one firm, enabling clients to potentially address multiple legal crises simultaneously. They offer free initial consultations and emphasize customized approaches to each client's unique financial or injury situation. The website highlights attorney experience and knowledge of available legal resources and remedies.

The firm operates as a traditional bankruptcy law practice with standard cost considerations typical of attorney-based filing services. While the website provides general educational FAQs about bankruptcy processes and types, it does not disclose specific fee structures, success rates, or attorney credentials. The breadth of services (mixing bankruptcy with personal injury) may indicate generalist rather than bankruptcy-specialist expertise.

Consumers considering bankruptcy should also explore alternatives. Debt relief programs may negotiate settlements for less than owed, while debt consolidation loans can simplify payments. Credit counseling agencies offer free financial assessments. After bankruptcy, rebuilding credit through secured credit cards and credit builder loans provides a structured path back. Credit repair services can help ensure accurate reporting. After discharge, qualifying for an installment loan can begin rebuilding payment history on your credit report.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 24/7 live call answering for urgent bankruptcy situations
Free initial consultations for bankruptcy and injury cases
Handles multiple debt types: credit cards, medical, tax, foreclosure, wage garnishment
Dual-practice model allows addressing bankruptcy alongside personal injury claims in same firm
Serves both individual and business bankruptcy clients
Provides educational FAQs explaining Chapter 7 vs. Chapter 13 bankruptcy differences
Assists with ancillary bankruptcy issues like divorce-related bankruptcy filing
cons: No published fee structure or pricing information on website
Combines bankruptcy law with personal injury practice, suggesting generalist rather than bankruptcy-specialist focus
No attorney credentials, years of experience, or case results disclosed
Website does not explain how firm prioritizes between bankruptcy and personal injury caseloads
No information on Chapter 13 plan success rates or typical client outcomes
best_for: Fort Worth residents facing overwhelming debt (credit cards, medical, tax) who need quick legal consultation
Individuals simultaneously dealing with bankruptcy AND recent accident injuries
Business owners considering business bankruptcy filing
People needing foreclosure defense or wage garnishment stops combined with Chapter 13 planning
diagnosis: Leinart Law Firm is best for Fort Worth-area individuals with significant unsecured debt (Chapter 7) or steady income (Chapter 13) who need immediate attorney guidance and prefer single-firm handling of bankruptcy plus any concurrent injury claims. Primary caveat: the firm's dual bankruptcy/personal injury practice structure suggests generalist rather than bankruptcy-specialist expertise, and lack of published credentials or case outcomes limits ability to assess comparative competency.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
